title: Infinite Loop Prevention
concept: Safeguarding against repeating the same tool call with the same arguments indefinitely; detect-and-break heuristics.

exercises.prompt: Last lesson: which is a clean reason to STOP an agent's loop?

options.text: It hit the max number of steps you set

exercises.explain: Agents should stop for clear reasons: goal done, too many errors, too many steps, or too much cost. A max-steps limit is one of those clean stopping rules.

cards.title: Stuck On Repeat
cards.body: Imagine a robot that keeps checking the weather, then checking it again, then again... forever. It never moves on. That trap is called an infinite loop.

cards.title: What Is an Infinite Loop?
cards.body: An infinite loop is when an agent repeats the SAME tool call with the SAME inputs over and over without progress. Loop prevention means spotting the repeat and breaking out of it.

cards.title: The GPS That Circles the Block
cards.body: It's like a GPS telling you to turn left, left, left, left around the same block. A good driver notices 'I've been here already!' and picks a new route. Agents need that same instinct.

cards.title: Catching the Repeat
cards.body: An agent searches 'best pizza' and gets no result. If it searches 'best pizza' again... and again... a detector notices the identical call and stops it after, say, 3 tries.

cards.title: Myth: More Tries = Better
cards.body: Trying the exact same thing again won't magically work. If the tool and inputs are identical, the result will be too. Smart agents change their approach or quit, not repeat blindly.

cards.title: Blind Repeat vs Detect-and-Break
cards.body: One agent loops forever; the other notices the repeat and escapes.

left.text: Calls the same search 500 times, wasting time and money.

right.text: Sees the same call repeat, stops after 3 tries, and reports back.

exercises.prompt: What is a 'detect-and-break' heuristic in an agent?

options.text: A rule that notices repeated identical tool calls and stops the loop
A way to make the agent run faster
A method to add more steps forever

exercises.explain: A detect-and-break heuristic watches for the same call with the same arguments happening again and again, then breaks the loop so the agent doesn't get stuck.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Repeating the exact same tool call with the exact same inputs will usually give the exact same result.
exercises.explain: Same tool + same inputs = same output. That's why blindly repeating won't help; the agent needs to change something or stop.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank about loop prevention.
exercises.sentence: A good agent tracks past calls so it can detect a repeat and break the loop.
exercises.explain: By remembering past calls, the agent can spot a repeat of the same action and stop before it loops forever.

exercises.prompt: Which line was most likely written by an AI agent explaining WHY it stopped?

options.text: I detected the same search call repeated 3 times with identical inputs, so I halted the loop to avoid wasting resources.
omg this is so boring lol im out
search pizza search pizza search pizza yum yum

exercises.explain: The first line is clear, structured, and explains the detect-and-break reasoning, just how a well-designed agent would report stopping an infinite loop.

exercises.prompt: An agent keeps calling the weather tool for 'Paris' every second and never finishes its task. Which fix BEST prevents this infinite loop?

options.text: Track recent calls; if the same tool and same arguments repeat too many times, break the loop and try a new approach or stop
Let it keep calling forever so it never misses an update
Make the weather tool respond slower
Add more identical weather calls to be safe

exercises.explain: Infinite loops happen when an agent repeats the same call with the same inputs and makes no progress. The safeguard is a detect-and-break heuristic: remember recent calls, notice the identical repeat, and stop or switch strategies. Paired with the stopping conditions from earlier lessons, this keeps agents safe, cheap, and reliable. Great work finishing Unit 482: The Agentic Loop!
